mcqs.question: What does Newton's First Law of Motion state?
options: A. A body at rest will stay at rest unless acted upon by an external force.
B. Force equals mass times acceleration.
C. For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction.
D. A body in motion will stay in motion unless acted upon by an external force.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an example of inertia?
options: A. A car accelerating.
B. A book lying on a table.
C. A person pushing a swing.
D. A ball rolling down a hill.

mcqs.question: What is meant by 'inertia'?
options: A. The tendency of an object to change its state of motion.
B. The resistance of any physical object to a change in its velocity.
C. The force that causes an object to move.
D. The speed of an object.

mcqs.question: If no net force acts on a moving object, it will:
options: A. Speed up.
B. Slow down.
C. Change direction.
D. Continue moving at constant velocity.

mcqs.question: Which of the following best exemplifies Newton's First Law?
options: A. A ball thrown upward comes back down.
B. A car stops when the brakes are applied.
C. A hockey puck slides on ice until friction slows it down.
D. A rocket propelling forward.

fill_in_the_blanks.question: An object at rest tends to stay at rest, and an object in motion tends to stay in motion unless acted upon by a ________.
The property of an object to resist changes in its state of motion is called ________.
Newton's First Law is also known as the law of ________.
If a net external force is applied to an object, it will cause the object to ________.
A stationary object will remain ________ unless a force acts on it.

short_questions.question: What is Newton's First Law of Motion?
How does inertia relate to mass?
Can you give an everyday example of Newton's First Law?
What happens to a moving car if no brakes are applied?
Why do passengers lurch forward when a bus suddenly stops?
